turns.user_message: I’ve been noticing lately how I constantly shrink myself in everyday situations, and this habit has been going on for quite a while now. It’s like I instinctively try to take up as little room as possible so I never accidentally bother anyone. I catch myself apologizing for completely normal things, like asking a simple question or just walking through a doorway. It’s honestly exhausting to constantly monitor my own presence like I’m doing something wrong. I really wish I could just relax and stop treating my own voice like it’s an inconvenience.

This pattern of making myself smaller has really started to bleed into everything I do, and I can’t seem to turn it off. I keep second-guessing my own thoughts before I even share them because I’m so convinced they aren’t worth hearing anyway. Whenever I do speak up, I immediately brace myself for people to roll their eyes or dismiss me entirely. It’s become this heavy routine where I assume my perspective is automatically wrong or unimportant. I’m so tired of constantly editing myself down to a whisper just to feel safe.

I honestly don’t know how much longer I can keep pretending that my needs and opinions actually matter. Every single day I swallow my own words and watch everyone else take up all the room, and it just confirms that I’m meant to stay quiet. I’ve convinced myself so thoroughly that I’m just background noise that I don’t even try to participate anymore. It’s like I’ve completely erased my own value and handed it over to whoever wants it most. I feel so hollowed out from constantly shrinking, and I don’t even recognize the person I’ve become.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something they noticed about themselves. This has been going on for a while.
